description: Baseline: 3,818µs combined, 24,881 allocs
Tag name interning via skip+byte dispatch: saves 878 allocs but verification loop slower than scan
String dedup (-@) for filter names: no alloc savings, no speed benefit
Streaming tokenizer: needs own StringScanner (+1 alloc), per-shift overhead worse than saved array
REVERTED: split-based tokenizer — regex can't handle unclosed tags inside raw blocks
REVERTED: split regex tokenizer v2 — can't handle {{ followed by %} (variable-becomes-tag nesting)
Merge simple_lookup? dot position into initialize — logic overhead offsets saved index call
Use Cursor regex for filter name scanning — cursor.reset + method dispatch overhead worse than inline bytes
Direct strainer call in Variable#render — YJIT already inlines context.invoke_single well
Array#[] fast path for slice_collection with limit/offset — only 2 alloc savings, not meaningful
TruthyCondition for simple if checks: -115 allocs but YJIT polymorphism at evaluate call site hurts speed
Index loop for filters: YJIT optimizes each+destructure better than manual indexing
Replace StringScanner tokenizer with String#byteindex — 12% faster parse, no regex overhead for delimiter finding
Confirmation run: byteindex tokenizer consistently 3,400-3,600µs
Clean up tokenizer: remove unused StringScanner setup and regex constants
Single-char byteindex for %} search: Ruby loop overhead worse for nearby targets
Regex simple_variable_markup: MatchData creates 374 extra allocs, offsetting speed gain
String.new(capacity: 4096) for output buffer: allocates more objects, not fewer
parse_tag_token without StringScanner: pure byte ops avoid reset(token) overhead, -12% combined
REVERTED: regex ultra-fast path for Variable — name pattern too broad, matches invalid trailing dots
Clean confirmation run: 3,314µs (-55% from main), stable
Regex fast path for no-filter variables: include? + match? overhead exceeds byte scan savings
